title: haplophyllum suaveolens var. cilicicum
intro: Haplophyllum suaveolens var. cilicicum, also known as Anatolian yellow spurge or Anatolian yellow spurge-leaved, is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, native to Turkey. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Haplophyllum suaveolens var. cilicicum include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and skin conditions, like eczema, as well as its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
